title: Power BI - Copilot Capabilities, Requirements, And Routing
applies_to: AI-assisted report creation and insights in Power BI

confirmed_facts.fact: The Power BI learning path includes a dedicated Copilot path covering Copilot overview, enablement, report creation, narratives, DAX generation, report chat, semantic model evaluation, prompting guidance, and data preparation for AI.
Power BI Copilot capabilities span chat-based analysis, DAX generation, AI-assisted report creation, and AI-written narratives.

Copilot requires administrator enablement in Microsoft Fabric and an organizational capacity such as paid Fabric capacity F2 or higher or Power BI Premium capacity P1 or higher; Pro or PPU alone is not sufficient.
Copilot isn't supported on trial SKUs or trial capacities and is not yet supported for sovereign clouds according to the requirements page surfaced by the Power BI Copilot documentation.

Using Copilot in Power BI Desktop requires access to at least one workspace on a paid Fabric or Premium capacity with Copilot enabled, and the tenant-level Fabric Copilot setting must also be enabled.

selection_rules: Use Copilot guidance when the user wants AI-assisted authoring, summarization, DAX help, or natural-language report interaction.
Do not route a request to Copilot-first patterns unless capacity, tenant settings, and supported-region prerequisites are satisfied.
Use normal report-authoring guidance instead of Copilot guidance when the environment lacks the required capacity or admin enablement.
implementation_notes: Treat Copilot as an accelerator layered on top of Power BI modeling and reporting, not a substitute for a sound semantic model.
Before planning a Copilot-enabled implementation, confirm tenant enablement, supported capacity, supported region, and workspace access.
If results quality matters, include data-preparation guidance. Microsoft explicitly links Copilot usefulness to prepared data and business context.
caveats: Copilot availability is environment-dependent and can fail for governance or capacity reasons even when users have normal Power BI access.
Some Copilot experiences are preview-only or have different workspace and billing behavior.
A Copilot-enabled environment can still produce weak results if the semantic model and business metadata are poorly prepared.
